description: Hillside letters originally reading 'Hollywoodland'. Griffith Observatory and Beachwood Canyon viewpoints frame telephoto shots.
facts: Close approaches are fenced; legal trails include Griffith Park ridges.
Helicopter tours circle the ridgeline for aerial angles.
history: Depression-era real estate promotion became a film-industry emblem after sign decay and partial removal.
significance: Global shorthand for the American entertainment industry.

description: Public observatory with Zeiss telescope demos, Tesla coil shows, and free-entry astronomy exhibits above the LA basin haze.
facts: Sunset crowds pack the terraces for downtown lights.
Parking fills on weekends; DASH shuttle links to Vermont Metro.
history: Griffith's will funded the building as a gift to the public; WPA-era murals celebrate science.
significance: Reinforced concrete icon of Depression-era civic optimism.

description: LA Philharmonic home with vineyard seating and organ pipes integrated into Gehry's curving wood interior.
facts: Guided architecture tours explain panel tuning after early glare issues.
LA Master Chorale seasons share the hall with visiting orchestras.
history: Lillian Disney's donation spurred decades of fundraising and engineering to realise the complex metal skin.
significance: Downtown's post-2000 cultural anchor beside the Music Center.

description: Pacific Park Ferris wheel and Route 66 'End of the Trail' sign draw sunset crowds; street performers line the boards.
facts: Bike path mile zero begins near the pier ramp.
Winter swells can close lower decks for safety.
history: Municipal pier survived private pier competition; carousel restoration keeps early 20th-century fairground DNA.
significance: Beachfront public space between Malibu cliffs and LAX flight paths.

architecture_style: White enamel travertine pavilions around central garden
description: European paintings, illuminated manuscripts, and decorative arts campus reached by tram from underground parking.
facts: General admission is free; timed vehicle reservation may apply weekends.
Cactus garden overlooks runways and Pacific marine layer.
history: J. Paul Getty Trust consolidated programs from Malibu villa to this fire-resistant hilltop archive and galleries.
significance: LA's most visited paid-admission-free art destination.

description: Universal Studios Hollywood — landmark in Los Angeles.
facts: Metro Red Line exits into CityWalk nightlife before the gates.
Express passes reduce queue time on peak summer Saturdays.
history: Lew Wasserman-era studio tour grew into a full theme park above the Cahuenga Pass.
significance: Studio-branded rides visible from the 101 freeway.

description: Venice Beach Ocean Front Walk — landmark in Los Angeles.
facts: Weekend parking fills side streets east of Pacific.
Green turtle sculpture marks the skate dance plaza.
history: Canals shrank but the boardwalk culture survived as LA's street theatre strip.
significance: Coastal counterculture beside Silicon Beach offices.

description: Antiquities and gardens modelled on Vesuvian villas reached by reservation from the PCH.
facts: Timed tickets separate from Brentwood Getty Center.
Herb garden interprets ancient Mediterranean plants.
history: Paul Getty's Malibu museum was rebuilt with stricter seismic and fire codes.
significance: Coastal classical collection distinct from the hilltop campus.

name_en: La Brea Tar Pits and museum
architecture_style: Mid-century museum and outdoor pit fencing
description: Active asphalt seeps with Ice Age fossil displays and re-created Pleistocene garden walks.
facts: Project 23 crates still yield bones from construction-era salvage digs.
Ice Age Encounters show runs in the 3D theatre on weekends.
history: Oil seeps trapped animals for millennia; Hancock expeditions turned pits into public science.
significance: Working fossil site beside the county art museum campus.

description: West-facing decks align telephoto shots of the Hollywood Sign over the Los Feliz ridges.
facts: Planetarium shows sell out on holiday weekends.
Sunset parking may require upper lots only.
history: Public telescope nights continue WPA-era outreach missions.
significance: Classic sign framing without illegal hillside trespass.

description: TCL Chinese Theatre forecourt prints and star plaques along crowded souvenir blocks.
facts: Street characters expect tips for photos.
Metro B Line exits at Hollywood & Highland.
history: Theatre district decayed then rebranded around tourism and metro reinvestment.
significance: Pop-culture pilgrimage spine toward the hills.

description: Space Shuttle Endeavour display with hands-on galleries and sketched mission timelines.
facts: Timed reservations still apply for shuttle pavilion entry at peak holidays.
Exposition Park Rose Garden sits west for picnic breaks.
history: Endeavour flew LA streets in 2012 before tilt-up into the vertical stack exhibit.
significance: NASA hardware anchor in Exposition Park museum row.

description: The Broad — landmark in Los Angeles.
facts: Check opening hours and ticketing on official sites before travel.
Crowds peak on weekends and public holidays.

description: The Petersen Automotive Museum is an automobile museum located on Wilshire Boulevard along Museum Row in the Miracle Mile neighborhood of Los Angeles. One of the world's largest collections, the Petersen Automotive Museum is a nonprofit organization specializing in automobile history and related educational programs. History Founded on June 11, 1994, by magazine publisher Robert E.
facts: Check opening hours and ticketing on official sites before travel.
Crowds peak on weekends and public holidays.

description: com-арена» (англ. com Arena, в 1999—2021 годах — «Стейплс-центр» (англ. Staples Center)) — многофункциональный спортивный комплекс в Лос-Анджелесе (штат Калифорния, США), открытый в 1999 году.
